free_text: Relative yields of the isomeric pairs 69g-Zn - 69m-Zn
and 52g-Mn - 52m-Mn in some spallation reactions
induced by 20-153 MeV protons
The circulating beam of the Synchro-
Cyclotron at the Gustaf Werner Institute for Nuclear
Chemistry.
Zinc isomers.
Zinc isomers.
Manganese
isotopes.
Manganese isotopes.
Manganese isotopes.
Na-24 decay data.
Several target elements were used, namely; manganese,
cobalt, copper, zinc, gallium, arsenic, bromine and
rubidium.
The target materials were of high purity and contained
no impurity likely to interfere with the results.
Flow-proportional counter.
No information about source of uncertainty
Irradiation time is unknown.
Depending on the form of the target a stack of
foils or a mixture of the powders was used.
007-010 Subents are corrected
Last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
ERR-T -> DATA-ERR.
Metalic foils of Zn were used.
To avoid difficulties in resolving the decay
curves the zinc samples were prepared by
electromagnetic isotope separation.

ERR-T -> DATA-ERR.
Metal pieces of Mn were used.
Aluminum.
Manganese.
Aluminum.
/by authors/ The yield of Mn-52m has to be corrected
for the growing-in from the mother nuclide Fe-52.
The cross-section of this nuclide was therefore
determined in some bombardments.
The decay curves of manganese samples were resolved
using a 'Biller Plot' after previous subtraction of the
contribution from the isotopes Mn-52g, Mn-54 and Mn-56.
The half-life of Mn-51 was taken to be 45.0 minutes.
This is an average of several determination of
half-life by authors.  Half-life uncertainty 0.3 min.
Tbl.4 from J.Inorg.Nucl.Chem.,19(1961)1
